data.title: Copper rises for first time in five days on China buying, disruption fears
data.description:
Copper climbed for the first time in five days on Wednesday as Chinese buying and concerns over disruption to sulphuric acid and diesel supplies supported prices, even as industry data showed the refined copper market was in a significant surplus.

Benchmark three-month copper on the London Metal Exchange was up 0.3% at $13,076 a metric ton as of 0940 GMT.

SP Angel analyst John Meyer said prices were being supported by concerns over shortages of sulphuric acid, which is used to make around 20% of the world's copper, due to the ongoing conflict in the Middle East.

"China banned acid exports and copper miners in Chile get a certain amount of their acid from China," Meyer said. Chile is the world's biggest mined copper producer.

Miners are also concerned about diesel shortages, a trader said, adding that Chinese consumers have been buying copper whenever the price dips, "especially ahead of a holiday."

The Shanghai Futures Exchange will close on Friday for the Labour Day holiday and not reopen until May 6.

Still, the global refined copper market was in a 276,000-ton surplus in February, the International Copper Study Group said on Tuesday.

"We see the refined copper oversupply in 2025 being sustained in 2026 and 2027," BNP Paribas said in a note.

The bank is nonetheless raising its copper price forecasts for both years, with a base case for prices to hit about $14,000 a ton this quarter, adding that funds' interest in the metal "continues to support prices well above fundamentally justified levels."

Nickel was flat at $19,435 a ton after earlier touching its highest since June 2024. The battery metal has been supported by production cuts in Indonesia, where the sulphur squeeze is also being felt.

Aluminium rose 0.2% to $3,544.50, not far off the four-year high of $3,672 struck on April 16, while zinc fell 0.7% to $3,345, lead inched up 0.1% to $1,957 and tin gained 0.9% to $49,405.

data.title: SiteOne Q1 sales miss estimates, co sees weather impact
data.description:
Overview

  • US landscape supply distributor's Q1 sales missed analyst expectations, organic daily sales fell 1%

  • Adjusted EBITDA for Q1 rose 14% and beat analyst expectations

  • Company completed two acquisitions and repurchased $20 mln in shares

Outlook

  • SiteOne expects 2026 prices to increase 2% to 3% for the full year

  • Company expects overall end market demand to be down modestly for full year 2026

  • SiteOne maintains 2026 Adjusted EBITDA guidance at $425 mln to $455 mln

Result Drivers

  • WEATHER IMPACT - Co said unfavorable weather across several regions led to lower sales volumes and a 1% decline in organic daily sales

  • PRICING AND MARGIN - Gross margin improved due to higher price realization and commercial initiatives, partially offset by freight and commodity deflation

  • ACQUISITIONS - Acquisitions contributed $12.4 mln to net sales growth for the quarter

Company press release:

Analyst Coverage

  • The average consensus recommendation for the diversified industrial goods wholesale peer group is "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for SiteOne Landscape Supply Inc is $167.00, about 16.8% above its April 28 closing price of $142.94

  • The stock recently traded at 31 times the next 12-month earnings vs. a P/E of 29 three months ago

data.title: Epiroc AB Q1 Profit Falls
data.description:
STOCKHOLM (dpa-AFX) - Epiroc AB (EPI_B.ST) released earnings for first quarter that Dropped, from last year

The company's bottom line totaled SEK2.104 billion, or SEK1.74 per share. This compares with SEK2.200 billion, or SEK1.82 per share, last year.

The company's revenue for the period fell 7.6% to SEK14.351 billion from SEK15.536 billion last year.

Epiroc AB earnings at a glance (GAAP) :

-Earnings: SEK2.104 Bln. vs. SEK2.200 Bln. last year.

-EPS: SEK1.74 vs. SEK1.82 last year.

-Revenue: SEK14.351 Bln vs. SEK15.536 Bln last year.

data.title: WAF: Q1 2026 marked a soft start, but strong AI server demand supports a stable full-year outlook
data.description:
Q1 2026 saw lower sales and profitability due to delivery phasing, but robust AI-driven server demand is boosting the outlook for leading-edge wafers. 2026 guidance remains cautious, with EBITDA margin expected between 20–24% and CapEx at EUR 180–220 million.

data.title: OGE Energy Q1 revenue beats estimates
data.description:
Overview

  • U.S. regulated electric utility's Q1 revenue beat analyst expectations

  • Q1 EPS fell yr/yr, impacted by mild weather and higher operation and maintenance expenses

  • Company maintained 2026 earnings guidance, assuming normal weather for the rest of the year

Outlook

  • OGE Energy maintains 2026 EPS guidance at $2.43, range $2.38 to $2.48

  • Company says 2026 guidance assumes normal weather for the remainder of the year

  • OG&E expects significant seasonality in earnings due to weather

Result Drivers

  • MILD WEATHER - Co said mild weather reduced electricity demand, impacting Q1 net income

  • HIGHER O&M EXPENSE - Increased operation and maintenance expense weighed on Q1 results

  • LOWER DEPRECIATION AND INTEREST EXPENSE - Lower depreciation and interest expense on assets placed in service partially offset negative drivers

Key Details

Analyst Coverage

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is 4 "strong buy" or "buy", 9 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell"

  • The average consensus recommendation for the electric utilities peer group is "buy."

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for OGE Energy Corp is $49.00, about 3% above its April 28 closing price of $47.59

  • The stock recently traded at 19 times the next 12-month earnings vs. a P/E of 18 three months ago

data.title: BRINKER INTERNATIONAL REPORTS THIRD QUARTER OF FISCAL 2026 RESULTS AND UPDATES FISCAL 2026 GUIDANCE
data.description:
(10:15 GMT) *Brinker International 3Q EPS $2.87 >EAT
(10:15 GMT) *Brinker International 3Q Net $127.9M >EAT
(10:15 GMT) *Brinker International 3Q Rev $1.47B >EAT
(10:15 GMT) Press Release: BRINKER INTERNATIONAL REPORTS THIRD QUARTER OF FISCAL 2026 RESULTS AND UPDATES FISCAL 2026 GUIDANCE

DALLAS, April 29, 2026 /PRNewswire/ — Brinker International, Inc. today announced its financial results for the third quarter ended March 25, 2026.

Third Quarter Fiscal 2026 Financial Highlights

"Chili's delivered its 20(th) consecutive quarter of same-store sales growth, up 4%, lapping a 31% increase a year ago," said Kevin Hochman, President and CEO of Brinker International. "Guest demand remained strong in the quarter, recovering quickly after significant weather headwinds in January, driven by continuous improvements in food, service, and atmosphere, along with unmatched everyday value."

Company sales were $1,455.5 million in the third quarter of fiscal 2026 compared to $1,413.0 million in the third quarter of fiscal 2025. Company comparable restaurant sales increased 3.3% in the third quarter of fiscal 2026, including 4.0% for Chili's. Chili's comparable restaurant sales for February and March both increased 5.9% with positive traffic, reflecting the underlying strength and momentum of the business. In contrast, Chili's January comparable restaurant sales of 0.6% were adversely impacted by Winter Storm Fern and one fewer operating day resulting from a holiday shift.

Chili's continued strong performance is driven by a disciplined strategy focused on improving the fundamentals of food, service, and atmosphere, supported by ongoing menu innovation, everyday value, and attention--capturing media and advertising that reinforce the Company's value proposition, drive trial among new guests, and strengthen loyalty. During the quarter, the Company utilized operational cash flow to pay the outstanding amount on the company's revolver and repurchased $108.0 million of the Company's common stock.

Financial results for the third quarter of fiscal 2026 and fiscal 2025 were as follows (in millions, except per share amounts and percentages):

The risks outlined in the Forward-Looking Statements paragraph of this press release, among other risks, could cause actual results to differ materially from forecasted results. We are unable to reliably forecast special items without unreasonable effort. As such, we do not present a reconciliation of forecasted non-GAAP measures to the corresponding GAAP measures.

Third Quarter of Fiscal 2026 Operating Performance

Segment Performance

The table below presents selected financial information (in millions, except as noted) related to our segments' operational performance for the thirteen week periods ended March 25, 2026 and March 26, 2025:

(2)  See Non-GAAP Information and Reconciliations section below for more 
     details

Chili's

  • Chili's Company sales increased primarily due to favorable comparable restaurant sales driven by menu pricing, partially offset by lower traffic.
  • Chili's Company restaurant expenses, as a percentage of Company sales, increased primarily due to unfavorable commodity costs and menu item mix, higher manager salaries, repairs and maintenance, delivery fees and to-go supplies, and other restaurant expenses partially offset by sales leverage.
  • Chili's franchisees generated sales of approximately $274.1 million for the third quarter of fiscal 2026 compared to $237.4 million for the third quarter of fiscal 2025.

Maggiano's

  • Maggiano's Company sales decreased primarily due to unfavorable comparable restaurant sales and unfavorable impact of restaurant closures. Unfavorable comparable restaurant sales were driven by lower traffic, partially offset by menu pricing.
  • Maggiano's Company restaurant expenses, as a percentage of Company sales, increased primarily due to sales deleverage, unfavorable menu item mix and commodity costs, higher delivery fees and to-go supplies, and other restaurant expenses, partially offset by lower hourly labor, manager bonus, and worker's compensation and general liability insurance.

Corporate

  • On a GAAP basis, the effective income tax rate was 18.4% in the third quarter of fiscal 2026. The effective income tax rate is lower than the statutory rate of 21.0% primarily due to leverage of the FICA tip credit. Excluding the impact of special items, the effective income tax rate was an expense of 18.7% in the third quarter of fiscal 2026.

Webcast Information

Investors and interested parties are invited to listen to today's conference call, as management will provide further details of the quarter and business updates. A real-time audio webcast of the presentation can be accessed via the Events and Presentations section of the Brinker Investor Relations page. The call will be broadcast live today, April 29, 2026 at 8 a.m. CDT:

https://investors.brinker.com/events-and-presentations/

For those who are unable to listen to the live broadcast, a replay of the call will be available shortly thereafter.

Additional financial information, including statements of income which detail operations excluding special items, and comparable restaurant sales trends by brand, is also available on Brinker's website under the Financial Information section of the Investor tab.

Forward Calendar

  • SEC Form 10-Q for the third quarter of fiscal 2026 filing on or before May 4, 2026
  • Earnings release call for the fourth quarter of fiscal 2026 on August 12, 2026

Non-GAAP Measures Brinker management uses certain non-GAAP measures in analyzing operating performance and believes that the presentation of these measures in this release provides investors with information that is beneficial to gaining an understanding of the Company's financial results. Non-GAAP disclosures should not be viewed as a substitute for financial results determined in accordance with GAAP, nor are they necessarily comparable to non-GAAP performance measures that may be presented by other companies. Reconciliations of these non-GAAP measures are included in the tables below.

About Brinker

Brinker International, Inc. is one of the world's leading casual dining restaurant companies and home of Chili's(R) Grill & Bar, and Maggiano's Little Italy.(R) Founded in 1975 in Dallas, Texas, we've ventured far from home, but stayed true to our roots. Brinker owns, operates or franchises more than 1,600 restaurants in the United States, 28 other countries and two U.S. territories. Our passion is making everyone feel special, and we hope you feel that passion each time you visit one of our restaurants or invite us into your home through takeout or delivery. Learn more about Brinker and its brands at brinker.com.

Forward-Looking Statements

The statements and tables contained in this release that are not historical facts are forward-looking statements within the meaning of Section 27A of the Securities Act of 1933 and Section 21E of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934. We intend all forward-looking statements to be covered by the safe harbor provisions of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. All forward-looking statements are made only based on our current plans and expectations as of the date such statements are made, and we undertake no obligation to update forward-looking statements to reflect events or circumstances arising after the date such statements are made. Forward-looking statements are neither predictions nor guarantees of future events or performance and are subject to risks and uncertainties which could cause actual results to differ materially from our historical results or from those projected in forward-looking statements. Such risks and uncertainties include, among other things, the impact of general economic conditions, including inflation, on economic activity and on our operations; disruptions on our business including consumer demand, costs, product mix, our strategic initiatives, operations, technology and assets, and our financial performance; the impact of current and potential tariffs and trade barriers; the impact of competition, including competitors employing our same strategies or discounting their offerings; changes in consumer preferences, including shifts in their brand preferences; consumer perception of food safety; reduced consumer discretionary spending; governmental regulations; the effectiveness of the Company's business strategy plan; loss of key management personnel; failure to hire and retain high-quality restaurant management and team members; increasing regulation surrounding wage inflation and competitive labor markets; the impact of social media, including the potential governmental ban of platforms used by the Company in its marketing initiatives; reputational damage or unfavorable publicity for our brands, which may result from actions of franchisees not within our control; reliance on technology and third party delivery providers; failure to protect the security of data of our guests and team members; product availability and supply chain disruptions; regional business and economic conditions; volatility in consumer, commodity, transportation, labor, currency and capital markets; litigation; franchisee success; technology failures; failure to protect our intellectual property; outsourcing; impairment of goodwill or assets; failure to maintain effective internal control over financial reporting; downgrades in credit ratings; changes in estimates regarding our assets; actions of activist shareholders; our pursuit of or failure to comply with new environmental and sustainability requirements; our pursuit of or failure to achieve any goals, targets or objectives with respect to sustainability matters; adverse weather conditions; terrorist acts; cybersecurity, artificial intelligence and phishing threats; health epidemics or pandemics; tax reform; inadequate insurance coverage; and limitations imposed by our credit agreements as well as the risks and uncertainties described in "Risk Factors" in our Annual Report on Form 10-K and future filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

Restaurant operating margin is not a measurement determined in accordance with GAAP and should not be considered in isolation, or as an alternative to operating income as an indicator of financial performance. Restaurant operating margin is widely regarded in the restaurant industry as a useful metric by which to evaluate restaurant-level operating efficiency and performance of ongoing restaurant-level operations. This non-GAAP measure is not indicative of overall Company performance and profitability because this measure does not directly accrue benefit to the shareholders due to the nature of costs excluded.

We define Restaurant operating margin as Company sales less Food and beverage costs, Restaurant labor and Restaurant expenses. We believe this metric provides a more useful comparison between periods and enables investors to focus on the performance of restaurant-level operations by excluding revenues not related to Company-owned restaurants, corporate General and administrative expenses, Depreciation and amortization, and Other (gains) and charges. Restaurant operating margin as presented may not be comparable to other similarly titled measures of other companies in our industry.

Reconciliation of Adjusted EBITDA (in millions)

Adjusted EBITDA is not a measurement determined in accordance with GAAP and should not be considered in isolation, or as an alternative to net income as an indicator of financial performance. Brinker believes presenting Adjusted EBITDA provides a useful measure of our operating performance, excluding the impacts of financing costs, capital expenditures and special items. We define Adjusted EBITDA as Net income before Provision for income taxes, Other income, net, Interest expenses, Depreciation and amortization and Other (gains) and charges.

data.title: Portugal jobless rate steady at 5.8% in March
data.description:
Portugal's monthly unemployment rate was 5.8% in March, unchanged from February and below 6.3% registered a year ago, the National Statistics Institute (INE) said in its flash estimate on Wednesday.

The labour underutilisation rate was at 9.8%, unchanged from the previous month.

The youth unemployment rate encompassing 16- to 24-year-olds stood at 18.1% in March, the lowest mark since October 2022, the INE said.

data.title: SiteOne Landscape Supply 10-Q: $940.1M Revenue, $(0.60) EPS
data.description:
SiteOne Landscape Supply reported quarterly net sales of $940.1 million and a net loss per share of $(0.60), reflecting a net loss attributable to the company of $(26.6) million. Revenue was essentially flat year‑over‑year with organic daily sales down 1%, while gross margin expanded to 33.9% driven by price realization. Operating loss narrowed versus the prior year as higher gross profit partially offset ongoing cost pressures.

Financial Highlights

  • Net sales: $940.1 million (Organic Daily Sales down 1% y/y; acquisitions contributed $12.4 million)
  • Gross profit: $318.8 million; gross margin 33.9% (up 90 basis points from prior year)
  • Operating loss: $(25.9) million (improved from $(29.5) million prior year)
  • Net loss: $(24.1) million (Net loss attributable to SiteOne $(26.6) million after non‑controlling interest)
  • Net loss per common share (Basic & Diluted): $(0.60); weighted average shares 44,586,582

Business Highlights

  • Revenue trend: Net sales were flat year‑over‑year; organic daily sales declined 1% due to adverse weather and softer new residential demand.
  • Pricing and margin actions: Continued price realization (approx. 2–3% guidance) helped drive a 90 basis point improvement in gross margin despite higher freight costs.
  • Channel and product mix: Agronomics segment grew roughly 2% driven by pricing; landscaping products declined about 3% linked to weather and weaker construction activity.
  • Acquisitions and network expansion: Completed multiple tuck‑in acquisitions (including Reinders, Bourget, French Broad and others) and opened a fifth distribution center to expand footprint.
  • Operational initiatives: Ongoing focus on supply chain optimization, category management, digital/B2B e‑commerce enhancements, distribution optimization, and improving branch productivity.

Original SEC Filing: SiteOne Landscape Supply, Inc. [ SITE ] - 10-Q - Apr. 29, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Rogers 10-Q: Q1 sales $200.5M; EPS $0.25
data.description:
Rogers reported first-quarter net sales of $200.5 million and diluted earnings per share of $0.25, driven by higher sales in industrial and electronics end markets and foreign exchange benefits; improved margins and cost actions helped lift net income to $4.5 million. The quarter included $5.9 million of restructuring and impairment charges related to a Germany consolidation and leadership changes. Cash remained roughly $196 million with planned capital spending of $30–40 million for 2026 capacity and R&D.

Financial Highlights

  • Net sales: $200.5 million, up 5.2% year-over-year (FX benefit of $7.9 million).
  • Gross margin: $64.6 million, 32.2% of sales, an increase of 230 basis points versus prior year.
  • Operating income (loss): $10.7 million, representing 5.3% of sales; includes $5.9 million of restructuring/impairment charges.
  • Net income (loss): $4.5 million, or 2.2% of sales, compared with a loss in the prior-year period.
  • Diluted net income per share: $0.25, versus $(0.08) in the prior year.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth drivers: Higher demand in industrial and electronics end markets supported overall sales growth; automotive demand softened.
  • Segment performance: AES rose 3.4% and EMS increased 7.0%, reflecting strength in electronics and industrial channels.
  • Operational efficiency: Margin improvement credited to higher volumes, a favorable product mix and consolidation of the Belgium footprint.
  • Cost and workforce actions: Announced restructuring tied to consolidating operations in Germany, along with leadership transitions and workforce reductions.
  • Liquidity and investment outlook: Cash balance remained near $196 million; capital expenditures planned at $30–40 million in 2026 to support capacity expansion and R&D initiatives.

Original SEC Filing: ROGERS CORP [ ROG ] - 10-Q - Apr. 28, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Wilmar's first-quarter profit slides on hedging losses, weaker associate incomes
data.description:
Singapore-listed Wilmar International reported a 23% fall in first-quarter core net profit on Wednesday, primarily due to hedging losses from market volatility linked to the Iran conflict and weaker contributions from associates.

The company said it expects these losses to reverse in the coming quarters, when physical transactions are settled.

Meanwhile, its associates and joint ventures across China, Europe and Southeast Asia regions made weaker contributions during the quarter, Wilmar said.

The food conglomerate said profits from its plantation and sugar milling segment also declined due to lower palm oil prices and production volume, as well as weaker sugar performance.

Wilmar posted a core net profit of $264.2 million for the three months ended March 31, down from $343 million a year earlier.

data.title: Diesel market structure eases further; front-month east-west spreads widen
data.description:
Asia's diesel markets eased again for a second straight session, with paper swaps easing into a narrower backwardation and cash differentials softening to a nearly two-month low, despite persistently thin spot activity on the trading window.

China's exports are still likely to be curbed for next month, with limited diesel volumes - probably below 100,000 tons - expected to regional destinations, traders say.

June spot sales could start emerging in the next week from other regional exporters, though discussion levels could ease further given the buyer unwillingness to pay higher prices for now.

Front month east-west price spreads were at larger discounts of $90-100 per metric ton, their widest since a week earlier.

Refining margins (GO10SGCKMc1) regained some ground slightly, recovering to a two-session high of around $58 a barrel.

Lower offers on the window weighed on 10ppm sulphur gasoil cash differentials (GO10-SIN-DIF), with values easing further to around $10.5 a barrel - though these levels were still higher than pre-conflict.

Jet fuel exports from China for next month will still mostly be to Hong Kong.

Meanwhile arbitrage price spreads between Asia and the U.S. west coast remained wide, at discounts of more than $15 a barrel, with freight costs at around $8 a barrel.

Regrade (JETREG10SGMc1) inched down further to premiums of $5 a barrel.

SINGAPORE CASH DEALS

- No deal for gasoil or jet fuel

INVENTORIES

- U.S. crude oil inventories fell for the second straight week, while gasoline and distillate stocks also declined, market sources said, citing American Petroleum Institute figures on Tuesday.

- Stocks for refined oil products at Fujairah in the United Arab Emirates have fallen to fresh lows of less than 7 million barrels, data showed on Wednesday, after the Iran war disrupted energy supplies in the region.

REFINERY NEWS

- India's Nayara Energy is expected to resume operations by mid-May at its Vadinar refinery in western Gujarat state after a maintenance shutdown, said Sujata Sharma, a joint secretary in the federal oil ministry.

NEWS

- China has approved 500,000 metric tons of fuel exports for May to regions other than Hong Kong, representing a near doubling of shipments forecast for April, trading sources said, but levels remain less than half of last year's corresponding averages.

- China's Sinopec on Wednesday said its refinery and petrochemical utilisation rates dropped in the first quarter as the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran disrupted feedstock supplies, but its chemical exports are set for strong growth this year.

- OPEC and its allies will lose some of their power over the oil market when the United Arab Emirates leaves the group on May 1, but the rest of the producer alliance is likely to stick together and continue to coordinate on oil supply policy, OPEC+ delegates and analysts said on Tuesday.

data.title: India stocks, rupee, swaps, call at 3:30 p.m. IST
data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex was up 0.79% at 77,496.36, while the broader NSE Nifty 50 index advanced 0.76% to 24,177.65, as stock-specific gains after quarterly earnings offset higher Brent crude prices on reports the U.S. may extend its blockade of Iranian ports.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee dropped 0.3% versus the U.S. dollar to 94.850, pressured by another rise in oil prices as efforts to end the Iran conflict stalled and foreign selling of Indian assets persisted.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN064835G=CC) was quoted at 96.47 rupees, with the yield up 1.43 bps at 6.998%.

OVERNIGHT INDEX SWAPS:

The one-year overnight index swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) was up 3 basis points at 5.99%, while the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) rose 3.75 bps to 6.63%.

CALL MONEY:

India's overnight call money rate (INROND=) was at 5.20% and the overnight TREPS rate (INTREPTOT=TCCL) was at 5.17%.

data.title: LXP Industrial Trust posts Q1 adjusted FFO rises; rev hurt by dispositions
data.description:
Overview

  • US warehouse REIT posted Q1 net loss, compared to profit a year earlier

  • Adjusted Company FFO per share rose 2.6% yr/yr

  • Q1 revenue declined, mainly due to property dispositions

Outlook

  • LXP sees 2026 net income attributable to common shareholders at $0.00 to $0.15 per diluted share

  • Company reaffirms 2026 Adjusted Company FFO guidance at $3.22 to $3.37 per diluted share

  • LXP says active leasing pipeline covers over seven million square feet

Result Drivers

  • PROPERTY DISPOSITIONS - Co said Q1 revenue decline was mainly due to property dispositions

  • LEASING MOMENTUM - Co highlighted 1.8 mln sq ft of new and extended second-generation leases in Q1, with base and cash base rents on these leases rising 19.1% and 11.9%, respectively

  • SAME-STORE NOI GROWTH - Same-store NOI increased 2.0% yr/yr

data.title: Bunge beats Q1 adj. EPS estimates, lifts full-year outlook
data.description:
Overview

  • Agribusiness and food ingredients firm's Q1 revenue missed analyst expectations, adjusted EPS beat consensus

  • Company raised full-year adjusted EPS outlook, citing improved processing and refining performance

  • Higher results driven by strong execution and improved market conditions, especially in South America and North America

Outlook

  • Bunge raises full-year 2026 adjusted EPS outlook to $9.00-$9.50 from $7.50-$8.00

  • Company expects higher 2026 results in Soybean and Softseed Processing, lower in Tropical Oils and Grain

  • Bunge sees 2026 adjusted annual tax rate at 22%-26% and net interest expense at $620-$660 mln

Result Drivers

  • SOYBEAN & SOFTSEED PROCESSING - Higher results driven by expanded production capacity and increased origination in South America and North America, per company

  • SOFTSEED VOLUMES & FOOTPRINT - Increased softseed processed and merchandised volumes due to expanded origination footprint and large crops in Canada and Australia, per company

  • OILS MERCHANDISING EXECUTION - Results from global oils merchandising activities increased, reflecting strong execution, per company

data.title: Middle East Crude-Dubai, Oman premiums fall; Murban steady
data.description:
The Middle East crude benchmark premium for Dubai fell to its lowest in nearly two months on Wednesday, while Oman's premium also declined and Murban held steady.

In recent weeks, spot premiums for physical crude have slipped from record highs hit earlier in the Iran war as refiners are drawing on inventories and cutting back processing to cope with disrupted Middle East supply.

However, some traders expect premiums to rebound if the Strait of Hormuz remains blocked and supply remains tight.

Oil prices rose nearly 3% on Wednesday, with the Brent crude contract hitting a one-month high, on media reports that the U.S. will extend its blockade of Iranian ports, likely prolonging supply disruptions from the key Middle East producing region.

SINGAPORE CASH DEALS

Cash Dubai's premium to swaps fell $3.89 to $7.41 a barrel.

Shell will deliver a June-loading Oman crude cargo to TotalEnergies following the deals.

NEWS

China has approved 500,000 metric tons of fuel exports for May to regions other than Hong Kong, representing a near doubling of shipments forecast for April, trading sources said, but levels remain less than half of last year's corresponding averages.

China's Sinopec on Wednesday said its refinery and petrochemical utilisation rates dropped in the first quarter as the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran disrupted feedstock supplies, but its chemical exports are set for strong growth this year.

Abu Dhabi National Oil Company has notified some customers that they could load two crude grades outside of the Gulf next month as the Strait of Hormuz remained closed, according to two people with knowledge of the matter and a notice reviewed by Reuters.

OPEC and its allies will lose some of their power over the oil market when the United Arab Emirates leaves the group on May 1, but the rest of the producer alliance is likely to stick together and continue to coordinate on oil supply policy, OPEC+ delegates and analysts said on Tuesday.

data.title: Silgan Q1 revenue beats on pass through of higher raw material costs
data.description:
Overview

  • US rigid packaging supplier's Q1 revenue rose 6%, beating analyst expectations

  • Adjusted EPS for Q1 beat analyst expectations

  • Company raised 2026 adjusted EPS guidance and reaffirmed free cash flow estimate

Outlook

  • Silgan raises 2026 adj net income per diluted share outlook to $3.73-$3.93 from $3.70-$3.90

  • Company confirms 2026 free cash flow estimate of about $450 mln

  • Silgan sees Q2 2026 adj net income per diluted share of $0.92-$1.02

Result Drivers

  • FRAGRANCE & BEAUTY DEMAND - Double-digit volume growth in dispensing products for fragrance and beauty markets drove segment performance

  • PET FOOD DEMAND - Double-digit volume growth in metal containers for pet food markets supported segment growth despite raw material cost inflation

  • RAW MATERIAL COST PASS THROUGH - Net sales increased primarily as a result of the contractual pass through of higher raw material costs

data.title: Humana Q1 adjusted EPS beats estimates, cuts GAAP EPS outlook
data.description:
Overview

  • US health insurer's Q1 adjusted EPS beat analyst expectations

  • Company affirms full-year adjusted EPS guidance, lowers GAAP EPS outlook

  • Q1 Medicare Advantage and state-based membership grew, aided by new contracts and acquisitions

Outlook

  • Humana affirms FY 2026 adjusted EPS guidance of at least $9.00

  • Company revises FY 2026 GAAP EPS guidance to at least $8.36 from at least $8.89

  • Humana expects FY 2026 Medicare Advantage membership growth of about 25% over 2025

Result Drivers

  • MEDICARE ADVANTAGE GROWTH - Individual Medicare Advantage membership increased, driven by new sales and improved retention from customer-led benefit strategy and service changes

  • CENTERWELL EXPANSION - Sequential growth in CenterWell Senior Primary Care patients, including from the MaxHealth acquisition, contributed to results

  • STATE-BASED CONTRACTS - Membership growth in state-based contracts was driven by new programs in Michigan, Illinois, and South Carolina

Company press release:

  • The average consensus recommendation for the managed healthcare peer group is "buy."

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Humana Inc is $205.00, about 10.8% below its April 28 closing price of $229.72

  • The stock recently traded at 21 times the next 12-month earnings vs. a P/E of 21 three months ago

data.title: SMOL: Net sales fell to zero, but operational progress and new financing support ongoing industrialization
data.description:
Net sales dropped to zero in Q1 2026, but net loss improved year-over-year. CNF-MIM capacitors showed robust performance in extended tests, and liquidity was strengthened through new loans. Ongoing efforts focus on industrialization, customer validation, and securing long-term financing.

Original document: SMOLTEK NANOTECH HOLDING AB [SMOL] Interim report — Apr. 29 2026

data.title: France's Constellium Q1 revenue rises on metal prices
data.description:
Overview

  • France-based aluminum products supplier's Q1 revenue rose 24% yr/yr on higher metal prices

  • Q1 adjusted EBITDA helped by record segment results and metal price lag

  • Company repurchased 1.2 mln shares for $28 mln and raised 2026 EBITDA, cash flow outlook

Outlook

  • Constellium raises 2026 Adjusted EBITDA guidance to $900 mln-$940 mln, excluding metal price lag

  • Company expects 2026 Free Cash Flow in excess of $275 mln

Result Drivers

  • HIGHER METAL PRICES - Revenue growth was primarily driven by higher revenue per ton, including higher metal prices

  • SEGMENT PERFORMANCE - Record adjusted EBITDA in P&ARP and A&T segments was driven by favorable price/mix, improved metal costs, and supply shortages of automotive rolled products in North America

  • METAL PRICE LAG - Positive non-cash metal price lag impact contributed $97 mln to adjusted EBITDA
